title: Missing security headers in HTTP response
summary: The HTTP response was observed missing one or more recommended security headers. The absence of headers such as Content-Security-Policy, Strict-Transport-Security, X-Content-Type-Options, X-Frame-Options, and Referrer-Policy reduces the application's defense-in-depth posture and may leave users exposed to cross-site scripting, clickjacking, MIME-sniffing, and man-in-the-middle attacks.
why_it_matters: Security headers instruct the browser to enable built-in protections that mitigate entire classes of attacks. Without Content-Security-Policy, injected scripts execute freely. Without Strict-Transport-Security, users may be downgraded to HTTP via SSL-stripping attacks. Without X-Frame-Options, the page can be embedded in an attacker's iframe for clickjacking. Without X-Content-Type-Options, browsers may MIME-sniff responses and execute uploaded files as scripts. These headers are low-cost, high-value defenses that complement application-level security controls.
how_to_fix:
Add the following security headers to all HTTP responses. Configure them at the web server, reverse proxy, or application middleware level:

1. **Content-Security-Policy** — Restricts the sources from which scripts, styles, and other resources can be loaded.
   Recommended: `Content-Security-Policy: default-src 'self'; script-src 'self'; style-src 'self'; img-src 'self' data:; object-src 'none'; base-uri 'self'; frame-ancestors 'self'`
   Adjust based on your application's requirements. Start with report-only mode.

2. **Strict-Transport-Security (HSTS)** — Forces browsers to use HTTPS for all future requests to the domain.
   Recommended: `Strict-Transport-Security: max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains`
   Only enable after confirming HTTPS works for all subdomains.

3. **X-Content-Type-Options** — Prevents MIME-sniffing attacks.
   Recommended: `X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff`

4. **X-Frame-Options** — Prevents clickjacking by controlling iframe embedding.
   Recommended: `X-Frame-Options: DENY` (or `SAMEORIGIN` if iframing within the same origin is required)
   Note: CSP frame-ancestors is the modern replacement but X-Frame-Options provides backward compatibility.

5. **Referrer-Policy** — Controls how much referrer information is sent with requests.
   Recommended: `Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin`

6. **Permissions-Policy** — Restricts access to browser features (camera, microphone, geolocation).
   Recommended: `Permissions-Policy: camera=(), microphone=(), geolocation=()`
unsafe_example:
HTTP/1.1 200 OK
Content-Type: text/html; charset=utf-8
Cache-Control: no-cache

<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>...</html>

<!-- No security headers present in the response -->
safe_example:
HTTP/1.1 200 OK
Content-Type: text/html; charset=utf-8
Content-Security-Policy: default-src 'self'; script-src 'self'; style-src 'self'; object-src 'none'; base-uri 'self'; frame-ancestors 'self'
Strict-Transport-Security: max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains
X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff
X-Frame-Options: DENY
Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin
Permissions-Policy: camera=(), microphone=(), geolocation=()
Cache-Control: no-cache

<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>...</html>
developer_notes:
Configure security headers at the reverse proxy or load balancer level (Nginx, Apache, Cloudflare, AWS ALB) to ensure consistent coverage across all routes. Framework-specific configuration:
- **Nginx**: add headers in the `server` or `location` block using `add_header` directives.
- **Express**: use the `helmet` npm package, which sets secure defaults for all recommended headers.
- **Django**: use `SecurityMiddleware` (HSTS) and `django-csp` for Content-Security-Policy.
- **Spring Boot**: configure via `HttpSecurity.headers()` in your security configuration class.
- **ASP.NET**: use `NWebsec` middleware or configure headers in `Startup.cs`.

Deploy Content-Security-Policy in report-only mode (`Content-Security-Policy-Report-Only`) first to identify and resolve violations before switching to enforcement. Use tools like securityheaders.com or Mozilla Observatory to audit your header configuration.
verification_checklist: Content-Security-Policy header is present with a policy that restricts script-src and other directives
Strict-Transport-Security header is present with max-age of at least 31536000 (one year)
X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff header is present on all responses
X-Frame-Options is set to DENY or SAMEORIGIN on HTML responses
Referrer-Policy header is present with strict-origin-when-cross-origin or stricter value
Security header configuration is tested with an automated scanner (e.g., securityheaders.com, Mozilla Observatory)

references.title: OWASP HTTP Security Response Headers Cheat Sheet
